name: fab
description: Floating action button anchored to a screen corner, with an optional speed-dial of secondary actions

meta.name: Fab
meta.description: Floating action button anchored to a screen corner, with an optional speed-dial of secondary actions

props.name: onClick
props.description: Called when the element is clicked.

props.name: actions
props.description: Speed-dial actions; each has a label, icon, onSelect, and optional disabled

props.name: defaultOpen
props.description: Whether the component is open on first render (uncontrolled).

props.name: onOpenChange
props.description: Called with the next open state when it changes.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class names merged onto the root element.

examples.title: Speed dial
examples.code:
<Fab
  label="Create"
  actions={[
    { label: 'New note', icon: <NoteIcon />, onSelect: newNote },
    { label: 'New folder', icon: <FolderIcon />, onSelect: newFolder },
  ]}
>
  <PlusIcon />
</Fab>
examples.description: The main button toggles a menu of secondary actions.

whenToUse: A single, high-emphasis primary action that floats above scrolling content (e.g. Compose, Add)
A small cluster of related create actions revealed from one corner via a speed-dial
Touch-first screens where the primary action should stay reachable at the thumb
whenNotToUse: Ordinary form or toolbar buttons — use Button or IconButton in the layout flow
More than a handful of actions — use a Menu, Drawer, or full navigation
Primary navigation between top-level destinations — use Dock

antiPatterns.bad: <Fab label="Save"><SaveIcon /></Fab> next to a form submit button
antiPatterns.why: A floating button competes with the in-flow submit and hides the action off the form

related.name: IconButton
related.reason: Use IconButton for an in-flow icon control; Fab floats and is high-emphasis

related.name: Dock
related.reason: Use Dock for bottom navigation between destinations rather than a single action

intent.a11yRationale: The main button is icon-only and requires a `label` that becomes its aria-label. With a speed-dial it exposes aria-haspopup="menu", aria-expanded, and aria-controls; the dial is a role="menu" of role="menuitem" buttons under vertical roving focus (Arrow keys, Home/End, wrapping). Opening moves focus to the first action and closing (Escape, outside press, or selection via DismissableLayer) returns focus to the button.

flexibility.note: Omit for a single-action button, or provide a speed-dial of secondary actions
Anchored to bottom-end or bottom-start; honours safe-area insets
